title: US's climate.gov site, taken down by Trump, relaunched by nonprofit
gist: The Trump administration took down climate.gov citing an executive order on 'Gold Standard Science', but former admins and volunteers relaunched the full 15-year archive as climate.us via a new nonprofit, exploiting the fact that federal works cannot be copyrighted.

insights.claim: The US government redirected climate.gov to NOAA.gov citing Executive Order 14303 ('Restoring Gold Standard Science') and a June 23, 2025 OSTP memorandum, effectively deleting the dedicated climate site.
insights.speaker: John Timmer
insights.so_what: Signals continued politicized removal of federal scientific resources, raising data-continuity risk for any business or research relying on government datasets.
insights.locator: Over decades, researchers in the US government

insights.claim: Volunteers exploited the fact that federal government works cannot be copyrighted to legally preserve and republish the entire climate.gov archive at climate.us.
insights.speaker: John Timmer
insights.so_what: Public-domain status of federal works provides a durable legal escape valve against political deletion of government data.
insights.quote: the federal government is prohibited from copyrighting
insights.locator: But the people who put together climate.gov

insights.claim: The climate.us team restored the full 15-year collection including news, expert blogs, visual climate indicators, maps, classroom materials, and the Fifth National Climate Assessment, and established a nonprofit for long-term public service.
insights.speaker: John Timmer
insights.so_what: Demonstrates a replicable nonprofit model for mirroring at-risk public datasets that institutions and investors may come to depend on.
